headline: Micron, Oracle and Semtech Are Getting Fresh Wall Street Attention as AI Infrastructure Demand Surges
summary: Wall Street is turning increasingly bullish on three AI infrastructure plays heading into a busy earnings stretch, with analysts raising price targets on Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) and Semtech (NASDAQ:SMTC) on the back of accelerating AI infrastructure demand. headline: Micron Stock Continues to Slide. Why Earnins Could Change That.
summary: Micron Technology  stock was on track for a third consecutive decline Monday as investors digested some mixed news ahead of the memory supplier’s March 18 earnings report.  The good: Analysts at two Wall Street firms reiterated Buy-equivalent ratings and boosted their price targets for the stock, arguing that the memory demand cycle is still on the way up.  Micron shares were down 1.2% to $365.81 in premarket trading.

headline: Micron’s AI Memory Milestone And India Expansion Raise Valuation Questions
summary: Micron Technology has begun shipping customer samples of a 256GB SOCAMM2 LPDRAM module, described as the world's highest capacity server memory module, aimed at next generation AI data centers. The company has also opened an advanced semiconductor assembly and test facility in Sanand, India, expanding its manufacturing footprint in Asia. These moves are positioned to support growing AI infrastructure needs and diversify Micron's global supply chain. For investors watching NasdaqGS:MU, these...

headline: Micron Stock Is Stagnating. Why Earnings Could Change That.
summary: Micron Technology  stock rose slightly Monday as investors digested some mixed news ahead of the memory supplier’s March 18 earnings report.  The good: Analysts at two Wall Street firms reiterated Buy-equivalent ratings and boosted their price targets for the stock, arguing that the memory demand cycle is still on the way up.  Micron shares were up 0.5% to $372.29 in morning trading.

headline: 5 AI Stocks That Could Be the Next Nvidia — Before Wall Street Figures It Out
summary: NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) has not solely become a trillion-dollar company because it made the best chips, but it became one because it was seemingly the only company that could deliver what the AI industry needed, exactly when it needed it.
